name: Colorblind Modes
description: A set of color and contrast settings that make gameplay, UI, teams, hazards, and feedback readable without relying on color alone.
design_notes: Colorblind modes work best when they combine palette changes with redundant cues. Define colorblind_filter_id, palette ids, contrast targets, preview content, and which UI or gameplay materials are affected. The setting should not recolor only the HUD while leaving critical world markers unreadable.
design_purpose: Make color-coded information readable for players with different color perception profiles.
Reduce reliance on color-only communication by adding icons, shapes, labels, and patterns.
Give developers a validation path for UI, VFX, minimaps, team colors, and puzzle states.
player_fantasy: The player feels the game communicates important information in a way they can reliably distinguish.

parameters.name: colorblind_filter_id
parameters.description: Selected filter or simulation profile used by UI and rendering systems.

parameters.name: color_palette_id
parameters.description: Palette definition applied to UI and gameplay markers.

parameters.name: contrast_ratio_target
parameters.description: Minimum contrast target for text, symbols, and critical indicators.

parameters.name: redundant_cue_policy_id
parameters.description: Non-color cue policy used alongside palette changes.

parameters.name: preview_scene_id
parameters.description: Preview content used to test the selected color settings.

parameters.name: material_override_scope
parameters.description: Scope of materials affected by palette or filter changes.

edge_cases: A color puzzle, team marker, or damage telegraph becomes unsolvable if the mode changes hue but not symbol language.
Post-processing filters can alter screenshots or streaming output differently from actual gameplay materials.
Local multiplayer players may need different color settings on shared or split screens.
A new content pack adds UI markers that do not use the palette registry.
common_bugs: Only menu UI uses color_palette_id while world-space markers keep hard-coded colors.
Contrast passes in English but fails after localization changes text length and background placement.
Team colors update on the scoreboard but not minimap, outlines, or damage numbers.
Preview screen shows safe colors but does not include actual hazards, puzzle states, or enemy markers.
balancing_notes: High-contrast outlines may improve readability for everyone, so competitive games should decide whether they are universal or mode-specific.
Do not use colorblind mode as the only fix for color-dependent puzzles; redundant shapes and labels are more robust.
Custom palettes need guardrails so players cannot make critical warnings invisible by accident.
accessibility_notes: Provide live preview with UI, gameplay markers, minimap, enemy/friendly indicators, and warning states.
Do not communicate critical states by color alone; include symbols, text, position, animation, or pattern differences.

implementation_notes.unity: Centralize color ids in ScriptableObjects or theme data and bind UI Toolkit, materials, VFX, and minimap icons to palette entries. A preview scene should apply colorblind_filter_id and redundant_cue_policy_id without requiring a full restart.
implementation_notes.godot: Store palettes in Resources and route Control themes plus world marker materials through the same color ids. Use live preview panels and avoid hard-coded Color values in gameplay scripts.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets, material parameter collections, and UMG style data for palette ids. Gameplay markers, team colors, and HUD widgets should all consume the same palette registry, with platform overrides applied at profile load.
implementation_notes.web: Use CSS variables and renderer material maps keyed by color_palette_id. Canvas, Phaser, and Three.js layers should share tokens, and preview components should include gameplay examples rather than only settings text.

relationships.reason: Damage labels and status colors need redundant shapes, text, or contrast when colorblind modes are active.
Color matching puzzles need non-color cues or palette alternatives so the solution is not encoded only by hue.
Map markers should use shape and icon differences in addition to color filters.

example_games.title: Overwatch 2
example_games.note: Includes colorblind and UI color customization options.

example_games.title: Fortnite
example_games.note: Includes colorblind mode settings affecting gameplay readability.
